title: Class action claims Meta AI glasses recordings used to train AI without users’ knowledge
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Meta Platforms Inc. and Luxottica of America Inc., alleging that Meta's AI smart glasses secretly transmit recorded videos to third-party contractors for AI model training. Plaintiffs Gina Bartone and Mateo Canu claim Meta misled consumers with "designed for privacy" marketing, while footage including sensitive moments was viewed and cataloged by human workers in Kenya. The lawsuit accuses Meta of violating consumer protection laws and seeks damages for all U.S. purchasers of the glasses.

title: Oracle Corp Stock (ORCL) Moved Up by 3.39% on Mar 23: What Signal Does It Send?
summary: Oracle Corp (ORCL) saw its stock rise by 3.39% on March 23, driven by significant growth in its cloud and AI sectors, exceeding market expectations for earnings and revenue. The company's remaining performance obligations reached an unprecedented level due to strong AI demand, leading to an increased FY27 revenue guidance. Despite positive analyst sentiment and institutional investment, the article notes investor concerns regarding high capital expenditures required for AI infrastructure and potential debt burden.

title: Pacifica Partners Inc. Has $2.99 Million Stake in Ciena Corporation $CIEN
summary: Pacifica Partners Inc. reduced its stake in Ciena Corporation by 25.8% in Q4, selling 4,225 shares but still holding 12,130 shares valued at $2.99 million. Despite this, several major institutions like Vanguard and JPMorgan increased their holdings, and institutional ownership remains high at about 91.99%. Insider selling has amounted to $34.37 million in the last 90 days, even as Ciena reported an earnings beat and strong revenue growth, with analysts maintaining a "Moderate Buy" rating.

title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Stock Drops 13% From All-Time High — Time to Buy the Dip?
summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) stock has fallen 13% from its all-time high, trading below $300 amidst a global market sell-off driven by Middle East tensions. Despite strong Q4 2025 earnings and revenue exceeding $400 billion for the first time, investor sentiment is impacted by the company's substantial 2026 capital expenditure plans for AI infrastructure. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" consensus with an average 12-month price target of $351.82, suggesting potential for significant upside.

title: The Backbone of the Billion-Dollar Brain: Broadcom Solidifies AI Infrastructure Dominance After Landmark March Earnings
summary: Broadcom Inc. has solidified its position as a key architect of AI infrastructure, with 44% of its revenue now AI-related, following a stellar March 2026 earnings report. The company announced a landmark partnership with OpenAI to co-develop a custom inference engine, signaling a significant shift towards specialized silicon and away from an Nvidia-only dominance in AI hardware. This move underscores the growing importance of high-speed Ethernet networking, where Broadcom leads, for massive AI deployments and future inference-centric workloads.

title: NextEra gets federal approval to build 10GW of natural gas power in Texas and Pennsylvania for data centers
summary: NextEra Energy has received federal approval to develop 10GW of natural gas power generation facilities in Texas and Pennsylvania to meet the growing demand from data centers. This development is linked to Japan's recent $550 billion investment commitment to the US market, with the projects to be jointly owned by both countries. NextEra plans to build and operate these plants, which include a 4.3GW facility in Southwest Pennsylvania and a 5.2GW facility in Anderson County, Texas, alongside investments in small modular reactors.

title: The AI Powerhouse: A Deep-Dive Research Feature on Vistra Corp. (VST)
summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) has transformed from a traditional utility provider into a critical infrastructure partner for the artificial intelligence industry, leveraging its nuclear fleet and integrated business model to meet the high demand for carbon-free electricity. Emerging from bankruptcy, Vistra has achieved significant financial performance, including investment-grade credit ratings and aggressive share repurchases, with robust future guidance. The company’s strategic shift, marked by acquisitions like Energy Harbor and landmark power purchase agreements with tech giants, positions it for continued growth amidst the electrification trend and increased need for grid stability.

title: Broadcom Inc stock faces pressure on Nasdaq amid AI momentum and dividend ex-date
summary: Broadcom Inc. shares traded lower on Nasdaq despite robust Q1 earnings, driven by strong AI demand and a significant revenue increase. The company's impending ex-dividend date and a new share repurchase program are key highlights for investors. Analysts maintain a bullish outlook, emphasizing Broadcom's strong position in the AI networking sector and its attractive yield for income-oriented portfolios.

title: Arista Networks (ANET) Targets AI Networking Boom with Expanding Market Opportunity and Strong Growth Outlook
summary: Arista Networks (ANET) is strategically positioning itself to capitalize on the AI networking boom by expanding its market opportunity from $60 billion to $105 billion. The company is focusing on innovative AI networking solutions, addressing infrastructure power demands, and strengthening its presence in the campus market through wired and wireless integration and open standards. With strategic partnerships and an increasing customer base, ANET anticipates revenues to exceed $10 billion, up from $9 billion last year.

title: Leaders and Experts from Amazon Web Services, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Meta, Dell, Applied Materials and AMD Headline Technology and Innovation Programming at CERAWeek by S&P Global, March 23-27 in Houston
summary: Leaders and experts from major technology companies like Amazon Web Services, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Meta, Dell, Applied Materials, and AMD will headline the technology and innovation programming at CERAWeek by S&P Global in Houston from March 23-27. The conference, themed "Convergence and Competition," will focus on the intertwining of energy and technology, exploring topics such as AI's impact on energy demand, data center development, chip design, and investment strategies. A new "Bridge" venue and the "Innovation Agora" will facilitate discussions and connections between energy and tech leaders, venture capitalists, and startups, addressing challenges and solutions for the future of energy.

title: Meet the Red-Hot Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stock That Has Crushed Oracle With 115% Gains in a Year. It Is Still Worth Buying Hand Over Fist.
summary: DigitalOcean (DOCN) has significantly outperformed Oracle (ORCL) in the past year, with its shares surging 115% compared to Oracle's 4% gain, despite Oracle's massive AI infrastructure backlog. DigitalOcean's business model focuses on providing cloud computing to developers, small to medium-sized businesses, and startups, offering a more cost-effective and simpler alternative to hyperscalers. The company is experiencing strong growth, particularly in its AI offerings, with an anticipated 21% revenue jump this year and 30% in 2027, making it an attractive investment for further upside.

title: Meet the Red-Hot Artificial Intelligence (AI) Infrastructure Stock That Has Crushed Oracle With 115% Gains in a Year. It Is Still Worth Buying Hand Over Fist.
summary: DigitalOcean (DOCN) has significantly outperformed Oracle (ORCL) in the past year, with its shares surging 115% compared to Oracle's 4% gain, despite Oracle's massive AI infrastructure backlog. DigitalOcean's success stems from its focus on small to medium-sized businesses and startups, offering an affordable and less complex cloud computing platform. The company's AI offerings are a major catalyst, driving substantial revenue growth and indicating strong future potential, making it an attractive investment.

title: Meta Stock Dips Amid Strong Revenue Growth & AI Investment Push | 2026 Analysis - News and Statistics
summary: Despite strong fourth-quarter revenue growth and robust operating income, Meta Platforms' stock has declined. The company's increased costs and expenses are attributed to substantial investments in AI infrastructure and talent, which outpaced revenue growth for the quarter. Meta's CEO anticipates further acceleration in AI investments, indicating a strategic long-term play amidst current market fluctuations.

title: State Street, Voya Seek Shelter From Default Risk
summary: Major money managers like State Street and Voya Investment Management are shifting from corporate bonds to mortgage bonds and other securitized debt. This move is a response to rising energy prices, inflation fears, and increased default risk in corporate debt, which has been exacerbated by geopolitical events and concerns over corporate profits. Mortgage bonds are seen as a safer alternative, offering better performance in "risk off" markets and potential benefits if interest rates fall.

title: Constellation Energy stock price drops 11% as $5 billion LS Power sale sharpens Calpine focus
summary: Constellation Energy (NYSE:CEG) saw its stock price drop 11% following a $5 billion asset sale to LS Power and a broader selloff in U.S. power stocks. The divestiture, mainly gas-fired plants, was mandated by regulators after Constellation's acquisition of Calpine, which is set to position the company strongly in the growing AI data center power market. Investors are now awaiting Constellation’s March 31 presentation for its 2026 business and earnings outlook amidst market caution and rising bond yields.

title: Oracle's AI Strategy: $50B Capex, Data Centers, and Market Response in 2026 - News and Statistics
summary: Oracle is making a significant pivot towards artificial intelligence in 2026, planning a $50 billion investment in data center infrastructure to become a leading AI solutions provider. This strategic shift moves the company beyond its traditional database and SaaS offerings to focus on specialized AI agents and sovereign AI solutions for governments. The substantial capital expenditure, funded through debt and stock offerings, has impacted its free cash flow, with Oracle experiencing negative cash flow of almost $44 billion in the first three quarters of the fiscal year.

title: This 1 ETF Keeps Outrallying the SPY, While Also Losing Less During Downturns
summary: The Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO) has consistently outperformed the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) over various timeframes, including a 24% return versus SPY's 16% over the last year, and 416% versus 222% over a decade. SPMO achieves this by systematically overweighting the highest-momentum stocks within the S&P 500 through semi-annual rebalancing. Despite concerns about momentum strategies during rapid market reversals, SPMO even demonstrated better resilience than SPY during the 2020 COVID crash.

title: The Greenbrier Companies (GBX) Secures $300M Railcar ABS Financing to Grow Leasing Business
summary: The Greenbrier Companies (GBX) recently completed a $300 million railcar asset-backed securities (ABS) offering through its subsidiary, GBX Leasing 2022-1 LLC. This financing, rated "AA" and "A" by S&P Global Ratings, is secured by over 17,000 railcars from Greenbrier's leasing fleet. The proceeds will support the growth of Greenbrier's leasing business and recurring revenue, reflecting strong investor confidence in the company's manufacturing platform and long-term strategy.

title: Evercore and Goldman Sachs Stay Bullish on Arista Networks (ANET)
summary: Evercore ISI and Goldman Sachs have maintained bullish stances on Arista Networks (ANET), a cloud networking company, due to its strong position in the growing AI infrastructure market. Evercore reiterated an Outperform rating and $200 price target, citing ANET's benefit from a major 6-gigawatt agreement between AMD and Meta Platforms. Goldman Sachs raised its price target from $165 to $188, maintaining a Buy rating, expecting strong double-digit growth in revenue and EPS driven by data demand, cloud migration, and network infrastructure investments.

title: PR News | On the Move: Rutgers Hires Prudential Financial's Quigley - Fri., Mar. 20, 2026
summary: Rutgers University has appointed Brian Quigley, formerly VP of government affairs at Prudential Financial, as the head of its governmental affairs office. In other moves, Capri Holdings Limited named Corey Moran as CMO at Michael Kors, and T-Mobile promoted Allan Samson to CMO, tasking him with transforming the company's marketing organization.

title: CleanSpark (CLSK)’s February Operational Update Reflects Continued Expansion Of Its Infrastructure Platform
summary: CleanSpark, Inc. (NASDAQ: CLSK) released its February operational update, showcasing continued expansion of its infrastructure platform. The company reported producing 568 BTC, reaching an operational hashrate of 50.0 EH/s, and acquiring a second site in Texas, adding 300 megawatts of ERCOT-approved capacity. This expansion strengthens CleanSpark's position as a digital infrastructure owner-operator, supporting its growth in blockchain, digital assets, AI, and high-performance computing.

title: Here’s Why Apple (AAPL) Could Be The Best Tech Stock to Buy Now According to Warren Buffett
summary: This article argues why Apple (AAPL) remains a strong investment, despite Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway reducing its stake. It highlights Apple's prudent AI strategy compared to other tech giants' high CapEx, and its successful diversification into services, which now account for 21% of revenue with nearly double the gross margins of physical products. The piece suggests Apple's large installed base positions it well for continued profitability even if iPhone sales plateau.

title: ARM’s Strategic Moves: Stock Appreciation Amid New Partnerships
summary: ARM Holdings plc (NASDAQ: ARM) has seen its stock appreciate by 5.19% due to positive innovation news and strategic partnerships with tech giants like Meta and Nvidia. These collaborations involve developing ARM-based CPUs for massive data centers and AI-focused PCs, leading to revised price targets from analysts and growing investor confidence. The company's financial overview highlights resilience, strong revenue, and significant demand for AI-powered solutions, positioning ARM for continued growth in evolving tech markets.

title: SAP SE Stock (SAP) Opened Down by 4.90% on Mar 20: What Signal Does It Send?
summary: SAP SE's stock dropped by 4.90% due to major concerns regarding its cloud migration, which is reportedly €2 billion behind target as customers prefer on-premise solutions. Further pressure comes from an ongoing EU antitrust investigation into its support options and security vulnerabilities. The company is now shifting its strategy to focus more on AI licensing to offset missed ERP modernization goals.

summary: Friday's market saw significant stock movements, with Tesla and Oracle experiencing declines, while Venture Global Inc. rose. The article highlights major stock movers across mega-cap, large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap categories, detailing their performance and notable news influencing their changes.

title: 7-Day Rally Sends Coterra Energy Stock Up 12%
summary: Coterra Energy (CTRA) stock experienced a 7-day winning streak, increasing its market cap by $2.8 billion to $26 billion and showing cumulative gains of 12%. Despite being deemed "Attractive" due to strong operating performance and moderate valuation, the article notes that investors should look for predictive signals rather than reacting to news after the fact. It provides a performance comparison of CTRA against the S&P 500 across various periods and highlights the company's key financial metrics.

title: Arm Holdings eyes full-chiplet CPUs, but analysts warn near-term headwinds remain
summary: Arm Holdings is expected to introduce its first in-house standalone merchant CPU at its "Arm Everywhere" event, marking a significant strategic shift. This move could substantially boost Arm's market presence and revenue by entering the $60 billion AI CPU segment. However, Bank of America analysts maintain a 'Neutral' rating due to near-term challenges like a slowdown in smartphone sales and increased competition.

title: Entergy AI Data-Center Deals to Save Billions for Louisiana Customers
summary: Entergy has announced power agreements for seven AI data centers in Louisiana, which are projected to save customers an estimated $5 billion over two decades, with $800 million directly benefiting Louisiana. Major tech companies, including Amazon, Google, and Microsoft, have committed to funding the necessary grid upgrades to prevent costs from being passed to residential customers, aligning with the Clean Energy Buyers Association’s Ratepayer Protection Pledge. This initiative is expected to significantly impact the region's economy and commercial land demand, though it faces scrutiny regarding potential delays and ratepayer implications.

title: Arista Networks vs. Broadcom: Which AI Infrastructure Stock Is the Better Buy for 2026?
summary: This article compares Arista Networks and Broadcom as AI infrastructure investment opportunities for 2026, highlighting their roles in data center networking. While both benefit from the AI boom, Broadcom is presented as the better buy due to its custom AI chip business and more attractive valuation. The article details each company's strengths, including Broadcom's leadership in networking hardware and ASICs, and Arista's strong EOS software platform and customer base.

title: Inside Meta’s New York flagship store: AI glasses, VR and immersive retail
summary: Meta has secured a 10-year lease for a 15,000-square-foot flagship store at 697 Fifth Ave. in New York City, solidifying its commitment to brick-and-mortar retail after a successful pop-up. The store, Meta Lab New York, follows the Los Angeles opening and will offer AI-integrated glasses, VR headsets, and limited-edition items, focusing on an immersive, community-oriented experience. This move aims to differentiate Meta Lab from traditional electronics retail and support its expanding physical footprint.

title: Arista Networks vs. Broadcom: Which AI Infrastructure Stock Is the Better Buy for 2026?
summary: This article compares Arista Networks (ANET) and Broadcom (AVGO) as investments in AI infrastructure, particularly in data center networking. While both benefit from the AI boom, Broadcom is presented as the superior buy due to its custom AI chip business and lower forward price-to-earnings ratio. The piece explains their respective roles in the networking ecosystem, with Broadcom making components and Arista assembling them with its EOS software platform.

title: Arista Networks vs. Broadcom: Which AI Infrastructure Stock Is the Better Buy for 2026?
summary: The article compares Arista Networks and Broadcom, two key players in the AI infrastructure networking market, to determine which is a better investment for 2026. While Arista Networks is strong in data center networking with its EOS software, Broadcom offers a broader portfolio including networking hardware, software, and a significant custom AI chip business. The analysis concludes that Broadcom is the better buy due to its explosive growth potential in custom AI chips and more attractive valuation, despite both companies riding the AI infrastructure wave.

title: There’s Still Hope for This Struggling Concentrated Growth Fund
summary: Polen Growth (POLRX), a concentrated growth fund, has underperformed its benchmark and peers for several years due to market headwinds, portfolio concentration issues, and stock-picking errors, particularly missing out on high-flying tech stocks. Despite these struggles, the article argues that the fund's strong management team, disciplined investment process focusing on quality companies, and recent adjustments to their strategy offer hope for future improved performance.

title: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) Climbs 10% as Funds Shift to Tech Stocks
summary: Applied Optoelectronics Inc. (NASDAQ:AAOI) saw its stock climb 10% on Thursday, closing at $101.92, as investors shifted towards technology stocks, particularly those riding the AI wave. The company also showcased new transceiver products for future AI systems, including 100G to 1.6T transceivers and a next-generation 400mW laser Continuous Wave for 25dBm ELSFP, emphasizing the reliability and performance crucial for demanding GPU fabrics and hyperscale AI infrastructure.

title: XLC Encounters Regulatory Uncertainty as Netflix-WBD Agreement Challenges Merger Activity
summary: The Communications Services Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLC) is facing increased regulatory uncertainty due to large mergers like the Netflix-Warner Bros. Discovery deal, which has heightened scrutiny and risk premiums in the sector. Despite being near its 52-week high, the XLC's performance is now contingent on the successful navigation of complex regulatory and operational challenges. Investors are advised to monitor the progress of major deals and regulatory developments as the sector's high valuation is vulnerable to M&A complications.

title: Oklo provides updates on DOE, NRC approvals
summary: Oklo has released updates on its progress with DOE and NRC approvals, including two nuclear safety design agreement (NSDA) approvals from the DOE for its Groves Isotope Test Reactor and Aurora Powerhouse, and a materials license from the NRC for its Idaho Radiochemistry Laboratory. While the company has achieved significant milestones, particularly in isotope production, it remains a few steps behind some competitors in the DOE's Reactor Pilot Program for certain projects. Oklo emphasized its current focus on construction, with the Groves reactor expected to be the first to come online.

title: PDD Holdings Inc Stock (PDD) Moved Down by 3.48% on Mar 19: Facts Behind the Movement
summary: PDD Holdings Inc. (PDD) stock declined by 3.48% due to regulatory scrutiny, anticipated financial results, and a tightening regulatory environment in China's e-commerce sector. Analysts, including Morgan Stanley, have re-evaluated PDD's outlook due to rising regulatory challenges and intense competition.

title: Vornado Announces Meta's 10-Year Lease at 697 Fifth Avenue, Manhattan
summary: Vornado Realty Trust (VNO) announced that Meta (META) has signed a 10-year lease for its Meta Lab New York retail flagship at Vornado’s 697 Fifth Avenue, Manhattan. This marks Meta's first flagship retail location in Manhattan, focusing on experiential retail for its AI glasses and virtual reality headsets. This lease deal highlights Vornado's strategy of diversifying property uses and securing high-profile tenants for its prime Manhattan assets.

title: Corning Taps Meta AI Deal And Foldable Glass For Future Growth
summary: Corning has secured a multiyear, $6 billion partnership with Meta Platforms to supply optical fiber and multicore solutions for AI data centers and introduced Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3 for foldable devices, with Motorola as an early adopter. These moves significantly broaden Corning's growth avenues into both AI infrastructure and consumer electronics. The company's stock has seen substantial gains, reflecting market excitement over these new product and customer developments.
